policyDecision: Keep current writable behavior temporarily, but require a later dedicated migration sprint to move decoration.screen_shader behind a display/render high-risk gate or otherwise resolve the mismatch.
decisionRationale: The row is currently writable and removing it from SAFE_WRITABLE_ROWS would be a behavior change requiring explicit approval.
Official source shows non-empty values are read and compiled as final screen fragment shader code, so normal path-only treatment is insufficient as a long-term policy.
Path validation alone is not display/render safety proof.
Compile-aware validation would require a dedicated shader validation design and must not use the live compositor or active render state.

rejectedPolicies.reason: Warning-only is insufficient long term because official source compiles shader content in the render path.
A high-risk gate is likely required, but this sprint does not change recovery behavior or migrate the row without a dedicated proof/migration plan.
Moving the row back to blocked would remove current writable behavior and needs explicit user approval in a behavior-change sprint.
